name: Razorpay Payment Flow — Circuit Breaker Protection
scenario: Razorpay's payment flow calls 3 external services: a card verification API, an SMS notification provider, and a fraud detection service. All three can fail under load. Build circuit breaker protection so payment processing continues even when non-critical services fail, with graceful degradation and proper fallbacks.
description: Resilience4j @CircuitBreaker on 3 service calls + application.yml config + fallback methods + CircuitBreakerRegistry event listener + @SpringBootTest with 4 tests verifying degradation behavior.

core.description: application.yml: 3 circuit breakers — 'card-verification' (failureRate=30%, window=20, wait=60s), 'sms-notification' (failureRate=70%, window=10, wait=15s), 'fraud-detection' (failureRate=40%, window=15, wait=30s). All with minimumNumberOfCalls=5.

CardVerificationService: verifyCard(cardToken) @CircuitBreaker('card-verification'), fallback: return VerificationResult.UNVERIFIED (allows payment to proceed with flag). VerificationResult record with status and verified flag.
SmsNotificationService: sendPaymentSMS(phone, message) @CircuitBreaker('sms-notification'), fallback: add to retry queue + log. Queue accessible for test verification.
FraudDetectionService: checkFraud(paymentId, amount) @CircuitBreaker('fraud-detection'), fallback: return FraudScore.LOW_RISK (allow payment, flag for manual review).

PaymentOrchestrator.processPayment(request): calls all 3 services, returns PaymentResult with status and list of degradation warnings. Payment completes as APPROVED even when non-critical services fail.

@SpringBootTest (4 tests): (1) payment completes when all 3 services fail, (2) SMS queued on notification failure, (3) UNVERIFIED flag set when card verification down, (4) CB OPEN after failure threshold. All 4 pass.
CircuitBreakerAlertService @Service: @PostConstruct registers state-change listeners on all CBs. AtomicInteger openEventCount incremented on CLOSED_TO_OPEN. Logs CRITICAL on OPEN.

bonus.description: Custom HealthIndicator 'razorpayCircuits': DOWN if payment-critical CB (card-verification or fraud-detection) is OPEN. DEGRADED if only sms-notification OPEN. Test: verify health DOWN when card-verification circuit OPEN.

Slow call detection on card-verification: slowCallDurationThreshold=2s, slowCallRateThreshold=50. Test: mock that takes 3 seconds, verify circuit eventually opens due to slow calls even without errors.
CircuitBreakerDashboardController @RestController /admin/circuits: returns JSON with all CB names, states, failure rates, slow call rates. @SpringBootTest MockMvc test verifying response structure.

data_to_use:
Test payment request:
  cardToken: 'tok_visa_4242424242424242'
  amount: ₹2499
  phone: '+919876543210'
  paymentId: 'PAY-TEST-001'

Expected degraded response:
  status: APPROVED
  warnings: ['card_unverified', 'sms_queued', 'fraud_check_bypassed']

Threshold: sms-notification failureRateThreshold=70%
  5 failures / 5 minimum calls = 100% → circuit opens after 5 failures
example_output:
# All services down — payment still approved:
[CB] Card verification down — returning UNVERIFIED
[CB] Fraud detection down — returning LOW_RISK (manual review flagged)
[CB] SMS notification down — queuing for U001
PaymentResult{status=APPROVED, warnings=[card_unverified, fraud_bypassed, sms_queued]}

# Circuit opens:
[ALERT CRITICAL] Circuit 'card-verification' OPENED! failure rate 35% ≥ 30%

# Tests:
✓ paymentApproved_whenAllServicesFail
✓ smsQueued_whenNotificationDown
✓ unverifiedFlag_whenCardVerificationDown
✓ circuitOpen_afterFailureThreshold
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. spring-boot-starter-aop is required in pom.xml — @CircuitBreaker silently does nothing without AOP. Fallback signature: must match original method's return type and parameters exactly, with Exception as the last parameter. @BeforeEach cb.reset(): reset circuit state between tests to prevent test interference. minimumNumberOfCalls: without this, one failure opens the circuit — set to at least 5. CircuitBreakerRegistry.getAllCircuitBreakers(): may be empty until first CB is accessed — @PostConstruct listeners can miss CBs; use registry.getEventPublisher().onEntryAdded() as well.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 4 @SpringBootTest tests pass. README includes: (1) architecture diagram showing 3 CBs, (2) what each fallback does, (3) why payment-critical CBs use stricter thresholds than notification CBs.
tips_for_success: AOP dependency: add spring-boot-starter-aop or @CircuitBreaker does nothing
Fallback return type: must exactly match — List for List methods, void for void
@BeforeEach cb.reset(): test isolation — circuits must start CLOSED
minimumNumberOfCalls=5: prevents circuit from opening on first test call
Test order independence: each test resets all relevant CBs in @BeforeEach
concepts_reinforced: Fallback method signature: same params + Exception last
Independent CBs per service: different thresholds for critical vs non-critical
Graceful degradation: payment succeeds with warnings when non-critical services fail
CircuitBreakerRegistry: programmatic state inspection and event subscription
